growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: MODG (MOD medium supplemented with 30 mM glucose)
growth_records.cite: Duport et al. TSPO safeguards porphyrin–iron balance under anoxic conditions in Bacillus cereus. mSystems (2026). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01738-25. [PMC13185565]
growth_records.snippet: µmax (h−1) 0.96 ± 0.05*; Y biomass/glucose (g·g−1) 2.01 ± 0.27; Yields (mol/mol glucose): Lactate 1.34±0.12, Acetate 0.19±0.02*, Formate 0.42±0.02, Ethanol 0.15±0.06 (Table 1, WT)
growth_records.notes: µmax=0.96 h-1 reported directly in Table 1 (anaerobic batch, MODG, pH 7.2, 37°C, modified Gompertz fit). Doubling time = ln2/0.96 = 0.72 h (calculated). Glucose uptake qS = µmax/Yxs = 0.96 h-1 / 2.01 g biomass/g glucose = 0.4776 g glucose/gDW/h -> /180.16 g/mol *1000 = 2.65 mmol/gDW/h (calculated). Product secretion qP = qS(glucose,mmol/gDW/h) x molar yield (mol product/mol glucose, from Table 1): lactate 2.65x1.34=3.55, acetate 2.65x0.19=0.50, formate 2.65x0.42=1.11, ethanol 2.65x0.15=0.40 mmol/gDW/h (all calculated, 2-step derivation). Note: the paper's stated Y biomass/glucose (2.01 g/g) is numerically higher than typical biomass yields (usually <1 g/g); value used as given without correction, so downstream derived rates carry that uncertainty.

growth_records.medium: MODG (MOD medium supplemented with 30 mM glucose)
growth_records.cite: Duport et al. TSPO safeguards porphyrin–iron balance under anoxic conditions in Bacillus cereus. mSystems (2026). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01738-25. [PMC13185565]
growth_records.snippet: µmax (h−1) 0.64 ± 0.14*; Y biomass/glucose (g·g−1) 1.86 ± 0.23; Yields (mol/mol glucose): Lactate 1.44±0.15, Acetate 0.28±0.05*, Formate 0.41±0.09, Ethanol 0.12±0.05 (Table 1, ΔtspO)
growth_records.notes: µmax=0.64 h-1 reported directly in Table 1 for ΔtspO mutant (anaerobic batch, MODG, pH 7.2, 37°C). Doubling time = ln2/0.64 = 1.08 h (calculated). Glucose uptake qS = 0.64/1.86 = 0.3441 g glucose/gDW/h -> 1.91 mmol/gDW/h (calculated). Secretion qP = qS x molar yield from Table 1: lactate 1.91x1.44=2.75, acetate 1.91x0.28=0.53, formate 1.91x0.41=0.78, ethanol 1.91x0.12=0.23 mmol/gDW/h (calculated, 2-step derivation same caveats as WT record re: Y value magnitude).
